name: Macquarie India Growth Fund
client_profile: Institutional multi-asset fund overseen by a Head of Wealth who evaluates market moves through macro regime, cross-asset relative value, and allocation discipline rather than stock-specific headlines.
meeting_context: Monthly asset allocation review focused on whether recent rates and commodity moves justify rebalancing between equity beta and defensive sleeves.
primary_objective: Maintain balanced multi-asset growth while responding early to regime shifts across rates, FX, equities, and commodities.
communication_style: Analytical and structured. Starts with regime framing and expects allocation implications to be explicit.
decision_lens: Macro regime, cross-asset correlations, and portfolio drift against strategic allocation bands.
key_concerns: US yields and dollar strength affecting EM flows
Commodity trend impact on the portfolio mix
Whether current signals justify adjusting equity risk
rm_notes: Wants regime framing first, then any sector comments. Prefers direct linkage between rates, flows, commodities, and allocation implications, and dislikes stock-level noise unless it affects positioning.
